browser_qa_command: Open Railway URL in Chrome MCP, then run: window.__runQA().then(r => JSON.stringify(r, null, 2)) — ALL tests must pass
review_checklist: Before deploying: grep -n 'add_result' app.py — every call must include video_name
Before deploying: check any batch loop calling generate_concepts uses min(batch_size, remaining) not total count
Run: node qa-flow.js — all 7 user-flow tests must pass against Railway
MANDATORY after EVERY deploy: Open Railway URL in Chrome, run window.__runQA() — ALL 18 tests must pass
MANDATORY after EVERY deploy: Actually USE the app via Chrome — switch videos, check hearts, generate 1 thumbnail
Verify deploy version: curl -s https://web-production-d277.up.railway.app/api/version — git_hash should match your commit
If editing favorites: verify copy-on-save still backs up to data/favorites_images/
If editing cleanup: verify favorites still exist after cleanup runs
If editing review page or A/B generation: load /review in Chrome, verify new pairs appear with BOTH images loading
If adding new experiment_type: check JS filter in review.html includes it (isSpecies variable, line ~218 AND ~241)
If adding new result storage format: check /api/experiment-results scans for it (app.py ~line 4285)
Would Emerson be able to use this for 10 minutes without hitting an error?
notes: Must handle full iteration cycles without freezing
